title: A cardboard tray, an assembly of the cardboard tray and a foil, a sheet of cardboard arranged for being folded into the cardboard tray
abstract: A cardboard tray (1, 101) folded from a sheet of cardboard (201, 301), an assembly of a cardboard tray and a foil and a sheet of cardboard arranged for being folded into a cardboard tray, wherein said cardboard tray comprises sheet portions defining a bottom wall (3) and side walls (5) defining a receiving space (31) of said cardboard tray, said side walls being connected, via respective first folding lines (7), to said bottom wall and flange parts (9, 11) connected, via respective second folding lines (13), to said side walls at a side of said side walls facing away from said bottom wall, wherein said flange parts are arranged such that neighbouring flange parts of said flange parts overlap in an overlapping region (15) thereof, wherein said neighbouring flange parts in said overlapping region are adhered to each other for realizing an uninterrupted flange (17) along a circumference of said cardboard tray, wherein said uninterrupted flange, apart from the overlap region, is formed of a single layer of said sheet of cardboard and is arranged for connecting a foil for closing said receiving space of said cardboard tray at an upper side of said cardboard tray.
claims:
Claims 1. A cardboard tray (1 , 101) folded from a sheet of cardboard (201 , 301), wherein said cardboard tray (1 , 101) comprises sheet portions defining a bottom wall (3) and side walls (5) defining a receiving space (31) of said cardboard tray (1 , 101), said side walls (5) being connected, via respective first folding lines (7), to said bottom wall (3) and flange parts (9, 11) connected, via respective second folding lines (13), to said side walls (5) at a side of said side walls (5) facing away from said bottom wall (3), wherein said flange parts (9, 11) are arranged such that neighbouring flange parts (9, 11) of said flange parts (9, 11) overlap in an overlapping region (15) thereof, wherein said neighbouring flange parts (9, 11) in said overlapping region (15) are adhered to each other for realizing an uninterrupted flange (17) along a circumference of said cardboard tray (1 , 101), wherein said uninterrupted flange (17), apart from the overlap region (15), is formed of a single layer of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) and is arranged for connecting a foil for closing said receiving space (31) of said cardboard tray (1 , 101) at an upper side of said cardboard tray (1 , 101). 2. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 1 , wherein said uninterrupted flange (17) is substantially parallel, preferably parallel, to said bottom wall (3). 3. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein said neighbouring flanges (9, 11) are adhered to each other in said overlapping region (15) via an adhesive. 4. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said flange parts (9, 11) are formed of a single layer of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301). 5. The cardboard tray (101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said side walls (5) are only connected to each other via said first folding lines (7) and via said second folding lines (13). 6. The cardboard tray (1) according to any one of the claims 1 to 4, wherein neighbouring side walls (5) of said side walls (5) are connected to each other via a locking part (19), wherein said locking part (19) is connected, via a third folding line (21), to one of said neighbouring side walls (5) and extends at least partly through a locking opening (23) provided in another of said neighbouring side walls (5) for said connecting. 7. The cardboard (1 , 101) tray according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein first flange parts (9) of said flange parts (9, 11) are connected to opposite side walls (5) of said side walls (5) and wherein a length of said first flange parts (5) corresponds substantially to a length of said second folding line (13). 8. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein second flange parts (11) of said flange parts (9, 11) are connected to opposite side walls (5) of said side walls (5), wherein said second flange parts (11) comprise a centre flange part (25) and connecting flange parts (27) provided at opposite sides of said centre flange part (25), wherein a length of said centre flange part (25) corresponds substantially to a length of said second folding line (13) and wherein said connecting flange parts (27) are extensions of said centre flange part (27) beyond said second folding line (13). 9. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 8, wherein said connecting flange parts (27) extend under an angle of substantially 90 degrees, away from said centre flange part (25) into said overlapping region (15). 10. The cardboard (1 , 101) tray according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said side walls define a height (H) of said cardboard tray (1 , 101). 11. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein each of said respective first folding lines (7) is interrupted by cut-outs (29) provided in said bottom wall (3) and said side walls (5). 12. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said uninterrupted flange (17) is directed away from said receiving space (31). 13. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 7, wherein a dimension of one of said overlapping regions (15) is in the range of 5% to 15%, more preferably in the range of 5% to 8%, of a dimension of one of said first flange parts (9). 14. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) has mass per unit area in the range of 200 gram/m 2 to 500 gram/m 2 , wherein said mass per unit area is determined according to ISO 536:2019. 15. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a stiffness of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) in a first cardboard direction is in a range of 8 mNm and 190 mNm, wherein said stiffness is determined according to DIN 53121 :2014. 16. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 15, wherein astiffness of said sheet of cardboard in a second cardboard direction (F), perpendicular to said first cardboard direction, is in a range of 8 mNm and 330 mNm, wherein said stiffness is determined according to DIN 53121 :2014. 17. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claims 15 and 16, wherein said stiffness of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) in said first cardboard direction is lower than said stiffness of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) in said second cardboard direction (F). 18. The cardboard tray (1) according to claims 6, 15 and 16, wherein said second cardboard direction (F) is substantially perpendicular, preferably perpendicular, to said third folding line (21) and/or substantially parallel, preferably parallel, to said locking opening (23). 19. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a thickness variation of said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) is in the range of 200 pm to 750 pm, wherein said thickness variation is determined according to ISO 534:2011. 20. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to claim 10, wherein a ratio of a width of said uninterrupted flange (17) and said height (H) of said cardboard tray (1 , 101) is in the range of 0.2 to 0.4, more preferably in the range of 0.25 to 0.35. 21. The cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a ratio of a surface area of the uninterrupted flange (17) and the bottom wall (3) is in the range of 0.3 to 0.45, more preferably in the range of 0.35 to 0.4. 22. An assembly of a cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the preceding claims and a foil, wherein said foil is adhered to said uninterrupted flange (17), such that said bottom wall (3), said side walls (5) and said foil bound a receiving space (31) of said cardboard tray (1 , 101). 23. The assembly according to claim 22, wherein said foil is adhered to said uninterrupted flange (17) by heating and pressing said foil to said uninterrupted flange (17). 24. A sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) arranged for being folded into a cardboard tray (1 , 101) according to any one of the claims 1 to 21 , said sheet of cardboard (201 , 301) comprising a bottom wall sheet portion (3), side wall sheet portions (5) connected, via first folding lines (7), to said bottom wall sheet portion (3) 

and flange part sheet portions (9, 11) connected, via second folding lines (13), to said side wall sheet portion (5) at a side of said side wall sheet portions (5) facing away from said bottom wall sheet portion (3).
